        territory of Australia (“Australian Users”).
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        The Company is operated as a Designated Internet Service under the
        Australian Online Safety Act 2021 (“Online Safety Act”, “Act”). This
        Appendix supplements the main Agreement and applies specifically to
        Australian Users. In addition to the provisions of the Agreement, Australian
        Users should be aware of and accept the following in full:
    </p>
    <h2>1. Definitions</h2>
    <p class="paragraph">
        For the purposes of this Appendix:
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        1.1. “<strong>Australian User</strong>” means a person accessing the Website
        and Services from the territory of Australia.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        1.2. “<strong>Class 1A Material</strong>” shall mean any material, by any
        medium, that depicts, or otherwise contains child sexual exploitation/child
        sexual abuse, extreme crime and/or violence, or material that is in support
        of terror and/or acts of terror.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        1.3. “<strong>Class 1B Material</strong>” shall mean any material that
        depicts or otherwise contains crime and/or violence, or drug-related
        material.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        1.4. “<strong>eSafety Commissioner</strong>” means the Australian eSafety
        Commissioner.
    </p>
    <h2>2. Compliance with Australian online safety laws</h2>
    <p class="paragraph">
        The Company operates in accordance with the Online Safety Act 2021 and any
        applicable Online Safety Codes and Standards registered under that Act.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        The Company may implement policies, systems, and procedures designed to
        prevent the distribution of unlawful and harmful content and to promote the
        safety of Australian users online.
    </p>
    <h2>3. Prohibited Content</h2>
    <p class="paragraph">
        Per the provisions of the Online Safety Act 2021, Users, Members and
        Verified Members hereby must ensure and subsequently warrants that:
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        3.1. The Content uploaded or attempted to be uploaded to the Website should
        be in adherence to our Agreement and specifically to clauses 3, 4, 5, 6 of
        the Agreement.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        3.2. Any audiovisual Content uploaded to, or attempted to be uploaded to,
        the Website must not contain Class 1A or Class 1B materials, including child
        sexual exploitation material or terrorist propaganda, nor must it contain
        content prohibited under the Agreement.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        3.3. Users, Members and Verified Members shall ensure, to the extent
        possible, that they comply with the provisions of this section.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        3.4. Users, Members and Verified Members are prohibited to distribute any
        Content, that:
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        a) promotes, incites, instructs in, or depicts serious crime or extreme
        violence;
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        b) includes non-consensual intimate images or sexual extortion material;
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        c) promotes self-harm, suicide, or dangerous activities likely to cause
        serious harm;
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        d) violates applicable Australian laws or regulations.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        The Company reserves the right to take action in accordance with clause 2 of
        the Agreement in the event of a breach of this section.
    </p>
    <h2>4. Age-restricted content and age assurance</h2>
    <p class="paragraph">
        4.1. Certain areas or content on the Website may contain Content and
        Materials, that are restricted to specific age.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        4.2. In accordance with clause 2.2.8. of the Agreement the Company may
        implement reasonable age-assurance measures, including but not limited to:
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        • age verification or age estimation technologies;
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        • restricted access systems;
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        • account verification processes;
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        • other technical or procedural safeguards.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        4.3. Users must be 18 years of age or older to access the Website and all
        types of Content.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        4.4. The Company reserves the right to restrict access where the age of the
        Australian User cannot be reasonably verified.
    </p>
    <h2>5. Safety, reporting and complaints</h2>
    <p class="paragraph">
        5.1. The Company has implemented technical, operational, and procedural
        measures designed to identify, prevent, and mitigate the distribution of
        harmful or unlawful material and to reduce the risk of harm to Australian
        children which may be caused by the distribution of relevant material. Such
        measures includes without limitation automated detection technologies, human
        content moderation, safety-by-design product development practices. These
        measures may be updated from time to time to address emerging online safety
        risks.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        5.2. The Company provides mechanisms enabling users to report content or
        conduct that may be unlawful, harmful, or otherwise inconsistent with the
        Agreement. Such reporting mechanism is available via our
        <a href="https://es.xhamster.com/info/contact">
            Contact us/Single point of contact
        </a> form. The
        Company will review and assess reported content and may take appropriate
        action, including content removal, restriction, account suspension or other
        actions as defined in clause 2 of the Agreement.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        5.3. More information about our reporting procedure, moderation practices,
        content upload requirements, technical and organizational safeguards can be
        found in our Trust and Safety policy
        (<a href="https://es.xhamster.com/info/trust-and-safety">https://es.xhamster.com/info/trust-and-safety</a>).
    </p>
    <h2>6. Role and functions of eSafety Commissioner</h2>
    <p class="paragraph">
        6.1. eSafety Commissioner is Australia’s independent regulator and educator
        for online safety. The main role of eSafety Commissioner is to ensure
        Australians have safer and more positive experiences online. eSafety leads
        and coordinates online safety efforts across Australian Government
        departments, authorities and agencies, and engages with key online safety
        stakeholders internationally to amplify its impact across borders.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        6.2. The main function of the eSafety Commissioner is to deliver
        comprehensive, compassionate and people-focused services. The eSafety
        Commissioner focuses on protecting users online, preventing the
        dissemination of illegal content, and making proactive systemic changes to
        online trust and safety. eSafety’s functions include:
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        • promoting online safety for Australians;
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        • receiving complaints and reports about certain types of harmful online
        material and conduct;
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        • administering regulatory schemes relating to cyberbullying, adult cyber
        abuse, image-based abuse, and illegal or restricted online content;
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        • enforcing compliance with the Australian Online Safety Act and applicable
        online safety codes and standards.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        6.3 Australian users have the right to make complaints directly to the
        eSafety Commissioner regarding any type of harmful online material or
        conduct that violates the Australian Online Safety Act. To do so, Australian
        users can access the eSafety Commissioner's contact form via
        <a href="https://www.esafety.gov.au/about-us/contact-us">
            https://www.esafety.gov.au/about-us/contact-us
        </a>
        .
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        Users are encouraged to report content through the Company's internal
        reporting tools first, where appropriate, so that the Company can review and
        address the issue promptly.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        6.4. The Company reserves the right to cooperate with the eSafety
        Commissioner in connection with compliance with the Australian Online Safety
        Act.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        This may include:
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        • responding to regulatory information requests;
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        • complying with removal notices or other regulatory directions;
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        • providing reports about safety measures implemented on the Service.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        6.5. Australian Users can find out more about the eSafety Commissioner, its
        role and functions, online safety protections and the reporting mechanism on
        the eSafety Commissioner’s official website - <a href="https://www.esafety.gov.au/">https://www.esafety.gov.au/</a>.
    </p>
    <h2>7. Australian regulatory compliance</h2>
    <p class="paragraph">
        Nothing in these Terms limits the Company’s ability to comply with
        obligations imposed under Australian law, including the Online Safety Act
        and any applicable Online Safety Codes or Standards.
    </p>
    <p class="paragraph">
        The Company may take any action reasonably necessary to ensure compliance
        with these obligations.
    </p>
